title: penstemon procerus subsp. formosus
intro: Penstemon procerus subsp. formosus, also known as the Formosan beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues. Penstemon procerus subsp. formosus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal uses.
